Details of gamma detection efficiency determination

Details of used methodology of neutron detection
Gamma Array for Inelastic Neutron Scattering (GAINS)
Neutron source was operated at 800Hz repetition rate
providing pulses of o1 ns FWHM.
Primary neutron production target is depleted uranium
Angle relative to electron beam - 90 deg.
For incident neutron energy. Flight path 198.7m.
data. Neutron energy resolution of about 1keV at 1MeV.
The data acquisition for the HPGe detectors was made
with Acquiris DC440 digitizers with 12bit amplitude
resolution and sampling rate of 440 million samples/s.
positioned at angles of 110 and 150 deg to the beam,
at distances of 14-16.5cm from the target.
Typical gamma energy resolution of 2.3keV for the
kBq on 1/6/1997) was used for calibration.
The measured efficiencies of 11 152-Eu gamma-rays of
energies 245-1408 keV were used for the tests of the
geometrical model (based on MCNP simulation of the
complete setup) for each detector. Average agreement of
1.70% between the calculated and the experimental
Detailed exp. efficiencies of each detector are given
in Table 4 of J,NIM/A,672,82,2012 for E-gammas 440,
upstream from the target, operated in parallel with the
HPGe setup - for neutron fluence measurement.
Fission chamber contains an assembly of 8 UF4 deposits
of 70+/-0.2 mm diameter, evaporated on five Al foils of
Thickness of the UF4 deposits of the fission chamber:
The amounts of U and 235U deposits were determined by
The P10 gas in the chamber is kept at atmospheric
The technique for the determination of the neutron
detection efficiency was revised according to the
methodology described in REL-REF of D.B.Gayther and
tests performed at the IRMM Van de Graaff accelerator
during 2009. The method was subsequently validated by
an instrument intercomparison carried out at PTB,
during which the neutron sensitivity of the fission
chamber(FP3/200m) was found to agree within one
standard deviation with the PTB proton recoil
- Na metallic sample(TP-NP08/10) was prepared at IRMM
from sodium ingot of elemental purity 99.8%, supplied
by Alpha Aesar. The material was formed by cutting
under argon atmosphere, rolling and punching under oil
into a cylindrical disk of 79.80+-0.08 mm diameter,
4.23+-0.08mm thickness and a mass of 19.44+-0.04 g;
enclosed in an Al can of 0.50+-0.01 mm wall thickness.
Sample had a density of 0.92 g/cm**3, 95% of the
The sample diameter is larger than beam diameter(61mm).

Ratios are averages weighed according to the energy

from the levels threshold up to the threshold of the
